arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system should ingest PDFs from a local `Docs/` folder, chunk and embed them, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use RAG to generate grounded answers via an LLM.

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql connector | Postgres requirement; pgvector gives vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET PDF text extraction, no native deps |
| ORM / data access | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | EF Core for chat history; Npgsql for Postgres |
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Already a .NET web SDK project; real-time chat UX |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, 1536 dims |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Good quality-to-cost ratio for Q&A |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│   ChatService    │────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat Page) │     │  (RAG pipeline)  │     │  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └────────────┘
                             │ query embedding
                     ┌───────▼──────────┐
                     │  Postgres +      │
                     │  pgvector        │
                     │  (chunks, chat)  │
                     └───────▲──────────┘
                             │ ingest
                     ┌───────┴──────────┐     ┌────────────┐
                     │ IngestionService │────▶│  Docs/     │
                     │ (PDF → chunks)   │     │  *.pdf     │
                     └──────────────────┘     └────────────┘
```

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── NavMenu.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   ├── Chat.razor          # Main chat UI
│   │   └── Home.razor          # Landing page
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs          # EF entity for chat history
│   ├── DocumentChunk.cs        # MEVD vector record for chunks
│   └── ChatbotDbContext.cs     # EF Core context (chat history)
├── Services/
│   ├── PdfIngestionService.cs  # PDF parse → chunk → embed → store
│   └── ChatService.cs          # RAG: embed query → search → prompt → respond
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf       # Product documentation (pre-existing)
├── Program.cs                  # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json            # Non-secret config (connection string placeholder)
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Install all required packages:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (MEAI abstraction)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` (OpenAI provider bridge)
- `OpenAI` (provider SDK)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` (vector store abstraction)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` (preview — chunking/ingestion if available, else manual)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Set up Postgres schema and EF Core
- Create `ChatbotDbContext` with `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- Create `DocumentChunk` as a MEVD vector record (Id, Content, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- Add connection string configuration
- Generate EF migration for chat history table

### 3. Build PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Extracts text per page via PdfPig
- Chunks text semantically (split on paragraph boundaries, ~500 token chunks with overlap)
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upserts chunks into pgvector via MEVD `IVectorStore`
- Runs on startup (or on-demand) to index documents

### 4. Build RAG chat service
- `ChatService`: accepts a user question + session ID
- Embeds the question via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Searches pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min score threshold 0.75)
- Constructs a system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Calls `IChatClient` with the augmented prompt
- Returns the response with source references
- Stores the exchange in chat history

### 5. Build Blazor UI
- `App.razor` / `Routes.razor` / `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor layout
- `Chat.razor` — interactive chat page:
  - Message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Input textbox + send button
  - Streaming response display via `GetStreamingResponseAsync`
  - Session management (new chat, history)
  - Source attribution display (which PDF page answered the question)

### 6. Wire up DI and configuration in Program.cs
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register vector store (pgvector)
- Register `PdfIngestionService` and `ChatService`
- Add EF Core DbContext
- Configure Blazor interactive server rendering
- Add health checks for OpenAI + Postgres

### 7. Apply guardrails
- Set temperature = 0 for deterministic Q&A answers
- Set `MaxOutputTokens` limit
- Pin model versions (`gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`, `text-embedding-3-small`)
- API key from environment variable / user-secrets (never in appsettings)
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)
- Source attribution in responses
- Logging of token usage (non-sensitive metadata only)

### 8. Test and validate

- Verify project structure is correct

# HelpChatbot — RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system should ingest PDFs from a local `Docs/` folder, chunk and embed them, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use RAG to generate grounded answers via an LLM.

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql connector | Postgres requirement; pgvector gives vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET PDF text extraction, no native deps |
| ORM / data access | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | EF Core for chat history; Npgsql for Postgres |
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Already a .NET web SDK project; real-time chat UX |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, 1536 dims |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Good quality-to-cost ratio for Q&A |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│   ChatService    │────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat Page) │     │  (RAG pipeline)  │     │  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └────────────┘
                             │ query embedding
                     ┌───────▼──────────┐
                     │  Postgres +      │
                     │  pgvector        │
                     │  (chunks, chat)  │
                     └───────▲──────────┘
                             │ ingest
                     ┌───────┴──────────┐     ┌────────────┐
                     │ IngestionService │────▶│  Docs/     │
                     │ (PDF → chunks)   │     │  *.pdf     │
                     └──────────────────┘     └────────────┘
```

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── NavMenu.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   ├── Chat.razor          # Main chat UI
│   │   └── Home.razor          # Landing page
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs          # EF entity for chat history
│   ├── DocumentChunk.cs        # MEVD vector record for chunks
│   └── ChatbotDbContext.cs     # EF Core context (chat history)
├── Services/
│   ├── PdfIngestionService.cs  # PDF parse → chunk → embed → store
│   └── ChatService.cs          # RAG: embed query → search → prompt → respond
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf       # Product documentation (pre-existing)
├── Program.cs                  # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json            # Non-secret config (connection string placeholder)
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Install all required packages:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (MEAI abstraction)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` (OpenAI provider bridge)
- `OpenAI` (provider SDK)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` (vector store abstraction)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataIngestion` (preview — chunking/ingestion if available, else manual)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Set up Postgres schema and EF Core
- Create `ChatbotDbContext` with `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- Create `DocumentChunk` as a MEVD vector record (Id, Content, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- Add connection string configuration
- Generate EF migration for chat history table

### 3. Build PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Extracts text per page via PdfPig
- Chunks text semantically (split on paragraph boundaries, ~500 token chunks with overlap)
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upserts chunks into pgvector via MEVD `IVectorStore`
- Runs on startup (or on-demand) to index documents

### 4. Build RAG chat service
- `ChatService`: accepts a user question + session ID
- Embeds the question via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Searches pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min score threshold 0.75)
- Constructs a system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Calls `IChatClient` with the augmented prompt
- Returns the response with source references
- Stores the exchange in chat history

### 5. Build Blazor UI
- `App.razor` / `Routes.razor` / `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor layout
- `Chat.razor` — interactive chat page:
  - Message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Input textbox + send button
  - Streaming response display via `GetStreamingResponseAsync`
  - Session management (new chat, history)
  - Source attribution display (which PDF page answered the question)

### 6. Wire up DI and configuration in Program.cs
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register vector store (pgvector)
- Register `PdfIngestionService` and `ChatService`
- Add EF Core DbContext
- Configure Blazor interactive server rendering
- Add health checks for OpenAI + Postgres

### 7. Apply guardrails
- Set temperature = 0 for deterministic Q&A answers
- Set `MaxOutputTokens` limit
- Pin model versions (`gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`, `text-embedding-3-small`)
- API key from environment variable / user-secrets (never in appsettings)
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)
- Source attribution in responses
- Logging of token usage (non-sensitive metadata only)

### 8. Test and validate
- `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify project structure is correct
- Validate DI registration and configuration

## Notes
- The project targets `net10.0` (already set in .csproj)
- `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- Chat history is stored in Postgres via EF Core (separate from vector data)
- Vector chunks use MEVD abstractions with the Npgsql pgvector connector
- No agentic workflows needed — this is straightforward RAG (MEAI `IChatClient` is sufficient)
- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, and a proper secrets manager

# HelpChatbot — RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system should ingest PDFs from a local `Docs/` folder, chunk and embed them, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use RAG to generate grounded answers via an LLM.

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql connector | Postgres requirement; pgvector gives vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET PDF text extraction, no native deps |
| ORM / data access | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres) | Chat history storage |
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Already a .NET web SDK project; real-time chat UX |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, 1536 dims |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (pinned) | Good quality-to-cost ratio for Q&A |

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│   ChatService    │────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat Page) │     │  (RAG pipeline)  │     │  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └────────────┘
                             │ query embedding
                     ┌───────▼──────────┐
                     │  Postgres +      │
                     │  pgvector        │
                     │  (chunks, chat)  │
                     └───────▲──────────┘
                             │ ingest

# HelpChatbot — RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system should ingest PDFs from a local `Docs/` folder, chunk and embed them, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use RAG to generate grounded answers via an LLM.

## Technology Selection

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql connector | Postgres requirement; pgvector gives vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET PDF text extraction, no native deps |
| ORM / data access | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | EF Core for chat history in Postgres |
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Already a .NET web SDK project; real-time chat UX |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, 1536 dims |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (pinned) | Good quality-to-cost ratio for Q&A |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │────▶│   ChatService    │────▶│  OpenAI    │

└──────────────┘     └───────┬──────────┘     └────────────┘

                     ┌───────▼──────────┐
                     │  Postgres +      │
                     │  pgvector        │
                     │  (chunks, chat)  │
                     └───────▲──────────┘
                             │ ingest
                     ┌───────┴──────────┐     ┌────────────┐
                     │ IngestionService │────▶│  Docs/     │
                     │ (PDF → chunks)   │     │  *.pdf     │

│   ├── PdfIngestionService.cs  # PDF parse → chunk → embed → store

- Temperature = 0, pin model versions, `MaxOutputTokens` limit

- `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly

- `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- No agentic workflows needed — straightforward RAG (`IChatClient` sufficient)
- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, secrets manager
